summary:
✅ **All tests pass!** 

The vulnerability was an **auth bypass via path manipulation**. The middleware was checking the raw URL path for the "/api/" prefix, but the router was cleaning the path (normalizing `//`, removing `..`, etc.) before routing. This allowed attackers to bypass auth checks using:
- `//api/admin` (double slash)
- `/health/../api/data` (path traversal)

The fix: Clean the path in the middleware using the same `path.Clean()` logic as the router, ensuring both layers check the same normalized path.
